title: Staehelina petiolata
taxonomy: Staehelina petiolata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. It belongs to the genus Staehelina and is native to the Mediterranean region. Staehelina petiolata is a relatively small plant, typically growing to a height of around 10-30 centimeters, and is often found in dry, sandy areas. The plant produces small, yellow flowers and has a distinctive petiole, or leaf stalk, which gives it its specific name. Its classification is further supported by its position within the larger clade of flowering plants, where it is closely related to other members of the Asteraceae family.
uses: Staehelina petiolata is a plant species known for its potential medicinal properties. It is said to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help in treating various health conditions. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Staehelina petiolata for medicinal purposes. More studies are required to unlock its full potential in treating various diseases.

The plant's leaves and flowers contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. intro: Staehelina petiolata, also known as the hairy staehelina or Mediterranean staehelina, is a flowering plant species in the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Staehelina petiolata include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Staehelina petiolata include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory infections.
